key_insight: A standardized CT preprocessing pipeline can convert heterogeneous TCIA scans into analysis-ready, reproducible inputs for radiomics and AI.
clinical_relevance: By reducing non-biological variation in imaging data, this framework can improve the robustness and comparability of computational imaging studies used in precision oncology.
method: Filter relevant CT series and convert DICOM to NIfTI
Harmonize orientation, voxel spacing, and intensity values
Integrate segmentation and validate metadata in a notebook-based workflow
evidence_signal: The pipeline was demonstrated on heterogeneous TCGA-KIRC CT data from TCIA, showing how it addresses variation in acquisition, metadata, and image quality.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on dataset-specific choices, and inconsistent source metadata or segmentation quality can affect downstream results.
paper_title: TCIA Radiology Image Processing for AI and Radiomics
